commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

commit.message:
fix: idempotent retry causes zero truth table writes

Move idempotency check to BEFORE any mutation in apply_transition(),
apply_prep_evidence(), and apply_prep_note().

Previously: dedup/conflict detection happened inside _append_validated_event()
which ran inside with self.transaction(). This meant the UPDATE on the truth
table (state change or evidence mutation) had already executed before dedup
was detected — silently mutating updated_at with no corresponding event.

Now:
- _check_idempotency() reads event_log OUTSIDE the transaction
- dedup   → _get_event_by_id() returns existing event, zero writes
- conflict → JSONL written, IdempotencyConflictError raised, zero writes
- new key  → UPDATE + _append_validated_event() proceed atomically as before
- _append_validated_event() simplified: assumes key is new, just INSERTs

New tests:
- test_dedup_causes_zero_truth_table_writes: proves updated_at unchanged on retry
- test_conflict_causes_zero_truth_table_writes: proves state + updated_at unchanged on conflict

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix traefik: correct flag name for cross-namespace middleware (v3)

allowCrossNamespaceMiddleware → allowCrossNamespace in Traefik v3.

commit.message:
Feat/user management improvements (#111)

* fix(calendar): eliminar eventos fantasma al arrastrar desde el backlog

- Añade callback eventReceive en ContentCalendar que elimina inmediatamente
  el evento que FullCalendar auto-agrega al soltar un elemento externo,
  evitando duplicados con los eventos reactivos de calendarEvents
- Mejora el manejo de errores en eventDrop: añade info.revert() para
  revertir visualmente si falla la llamada a la API
- Añade función removeDisc en DiscCalendar para limpiar el estado local
  al eliminar un disco del calendario
- Añade función handleDateChange en DiscCalendar para el cambio de fecha
- Mejora el manejo de errores en DiscComponent.deleteDiscFunction: muestra
  mensaje específico para errores 404 (elemento fantasma)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(calendar): permitir borrar eventos radar sin lista y mostrar fechas

- Muestra publicationDate y closeDate del evento en la cabecera del modal
  de radar/best como info de solo lectura (DD/MM/YYYY → DD/MM/YYYY)
- Añade botón "Borrar Evento" en el modal cuando el radar no tiene lista
  asociada (list: null), emitiendo el evento delete que abre el modal de
  confirmación estándar vía confirmDeleteContent
- Mantiene "Borrar Lista" e "Ir al Detalle" solo cuando existe lista

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(calendar): añadir selector de autor en modal de radar/best

Añade selector de autor al RadarActionsModal (único modal que no lo tenía).
Al cambiar el autor se llama updateContent con solo el authorId y se
actualiza el estado local de forma optimista sin recargar toda la vista.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(calendar): corregir desfase de fechas entre modal y calendario

- formatContentDate usa split('T')[0] en lugar de new Date() para evitar
  conversiones de zona horaria que desplazan el día mostrado
- toCalendarDate convierte fechas "YYYY-MM-DD" de FullCalendar a
  "YYYY-MM-DDT18:00:00.000Z" antes de enviarlas al backend, evitando
  desfases en zonas horarias UTC- al almacenar a medianoche UTC
- Aplica en handleDropToCalendar (drag desde backlog) y eventDrop
  (arrastrar entre días del calendario)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(calendar): fechas del modal radar son inicio/fin del evento en calendario

Reemplaza los inputs de listDate/closeDate (fechas internas de la lista)
por inputs de publicationDate/closeDate del content (inicio y fin del
evento tal como aparece en el calendario). Al cambiar una fecha se llama
updateContent con T18:00:00.000Z y se actualiza el estado local.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(calendar): corregir cálculo de fecha fin en eventos de varios días

new Date("YYYY-MM-DD") parsea como medianoche UTC. En UTC+1, setDate()
opera en hora local y el resultado en UTC sigue siendo el día anterior,
haciendo que los eventos de 2 días solo muestren 1 día en el calendario.

Solución: usar T12:00:00.000Z y setUTCDate para operar siempre en UTC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(content-calendar): migrate backlog to boolean field

Use the new backlog boolean field from the API instead of relying on
publicationDate being null. Drag to calendar sends backlog:false,
drag to backlog panel sends backlog:true. Backlog loading now uses
GET /contents?backlog=true directly.

Sealed format, Solana lore, Mystic/Royal/Chaos talent descriptions, Figment type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: match correct prompt text in expect for version:set

The pattern "*version*" matched "Proceeding with outdated version."
(EAS CLI's update notice) before the actual prompt appeared, causing
send to fire prematurely and the script to hang.

Changed to "*would you like*" which uniquely matches the prompt text
"What version would you like to set?" from the prompts npm library.

Also added 30s timeout to prevent indefinite hangs.

  trusted-dealer key causes "invalid put" on root contract updates)
- Fix 10 Dioxus hook violations: use_node_action() called inside event
  handlers instead of component body (causes runtime panic)
- Add UpdateResponse and Node error console logging in WASM
- E2E test 23: root grants admin, non-root admin sees restricted view
- E2E test 24: root saves toll rates, other user sees updated rates
- 43/43 E2E tests pass (idempotent, verified twice)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix affiliations parser: strip leading numbers from all fields

stripLeadingNumber was only applied to department. Hospital, city
and country could still carry PubMed numeric author-affiliation
prefixes (e.g. "2Amrita Advanced Centre..."). Now applied to all
four fields at return.

SQL backfill for existing data (run in Supabase SQL Editor):
  UPDATE authors SET department = regexp_replace(department, '^\d+\s*', '') WHERE department ~ '^\d';
  UPDATE authors SET hospital   = regexp_replace(hospital,   '^\d+\s*', '') WHERE hospital   ~ '^\d';

commit.message:
Condense CLAUDE.md and extract commands reference

Move all command examples to docs/commands.md for full documentation.
CLAUDE.md keeps key commands inline and references the full file.
Merge Development Workflow and Commit Policy sections, deduplicate

commit.message:
feat: replace enable_thinking with granular reasoning_effort enum (#65)

Replace the boolean enable_thinking flag with a ReasoningEffort enum
(low/medium/high/null) for fine-grained control over reasoning depth.

Full-stack changes:
- New ReasoningEffort enum (Rust) and type (TypeScript)
- DB migration: enable_thinking -> reasoning_effort with ASSERT validation
- LLMProvider trait updated: reasoning_effort: Option<ReasoningEffort>
- All 3 providers adapted (OpenAI-compat, Ollama, Mistral)
- thinking_tokens field added to Message, StreamChunk, metrics
- extract_thinking_from_message() utility for response parsing
- AgentForm: reasoning effort dropdown (conditional on is_reasoning model)
- MessageMetrics: BrainCircuit icon with thinking token count
- Dead code removed: execute_workflow, test_llm_completion, ProviderManager::complete()

Key design decision: reasoning_effort is intentionally not passed during
tool-loop iterations as most providers cannot use thinking and tools
simultaneously.

commit.message:
feat: Add Command Center v2 API endpoints with Signal Engine

Implements 4 new /api/command/* endpoints for living Command Center:

ENDPOINTS:
- GET /summary - Dashboard aggregation (signals + scenarios + health)
- GET /scenarios - Enhanced list with prob/conf separation
- GET /scenario/:id - Evidence packet + WHY breakdown
- POST /simulate - What-if scenario calculator

ARCHITECTURE:
- Signal Engine v2.0 (lib/scenarios/signalEngine.ts)
  - Probability: likelihood calculation with recency decay
  - Confidence: assessment certainty (quality + diversity + verification)
  - Trend: rising/stable/falling detection
  - Evidence: traceability to feed_items

- Data Health System (lib/db/migrations/data_health_schema.sql)
  - Health scoring (0-100) per source
  - Staleness detection (>2h stale, >24h dead)
  - Latency tracking + recovery detection

KEY FEATURES:
- Probability ≠ Confidence (SEPARATED as required)
- Unix SECONDS timestamps (not milliseconds)
- TypeScript strict mode (no any)
- Baseline monitoring mode (no signals = normal, not error)
- JSON field handling for SQLite/PostgreSQL compatibility

FIXES:
- Next.js 16 async params pattern
- ScenarioState field naming (delta_6h, delta_24h)

TEAM CONTRIBUTION:
- signal-architect: Signal Engine design + implementation (S+)
- health-monitor: Data Health system + migration (S+)
- db-auditor: Infrastructure audit (S+)
- api-builder: 4 API endpoints implementation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
